content.problemStatement:
There is a long long highway leading to Chef's new restaurant. But the problem is that almost no one stops in it, hence it's even unprofitable to hold it now. The only idea Chef has is that the restaurant has not been very well advertised, so it has no clients just because nobody knows about it! In order to change the situation Chef has decided to use some billboards along the highway for advertising.




There are exactly N billboards along the highway. Chef may place advertisements at any subset of billboards. To prevent casual drivers from forgetting about how good the restaurant is, there should be at least K restaurant's advertisement among every M consecutive billboards. But still each billboard's time costs some money, and Chef would like to pay the least amount possible (after all, he has already spent a lot of money for the restaurant at no profit). Now you are to help Chef count the number of different ways to place the minimal number of advertisements still meeting the condition above.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains three integers N, M, and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of different ways to place the minimal number of advertisements still meeting the condition above, modulo 1,000,000,007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, there is only one way to place the advertisement, which is to place it on the first billboard. For the second test case, there are three ways to place the advertisements: on the first and third billboards, on the second and third billboards, or on the second and fourth billboards. For the third test case, there are six ways to place the advertisements: on the first, third, and fifth billboards, on the first, third, and sixth billboards, on the first, fourth, and fifth billboards, on the first, fourth, and sixth billboards, on the second, fourth, and fifth billboards, or on the second, fourth, and sixth billboards.

For the first test case, there are six ways to place the advertisements: on the first, third, and fifth billboards, on the first, third, and sixth billboards, on the first, fourth, and fifth billboards, on the first, fourth, and sixth billboards, on the second, fourth, and fifth billboards, or on the second, fourth, and sixth billboards. For the second test case, there are 126 ways to place the advertisements.
For the test case, there are 100 ways to place the advertisements.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. We maintain a DP array where dp[i] represents the number of ways to place the minimal number of advertisements up to the ith billboard. We iterate over each billboard and for each billboard, we consider all possible previous billboards and calculate the number of ways to place the minimal number of advertisements.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * M) per test case, O(T * M) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(M) — the dominant structure is the DP array.
